title: Koyfin Pricing Explained: All Plans, Costs & Fees (2026)

excerpt: Complete breakdown of Koyfin pricing — all plans, hidden fees, and how to save money.
content:
Koyfin Pricing Overview

Koyfin positions itself as the retail investor's answer to Bloomberg Terminal—offering institutional-grade financial data, fundamental analysis tools, and macro indicators without the six-figure annual price tag. Founded in 2016 and headquartered in New York, Koyfin has built a reputation for depth across asset classes: stocks, ETFs, forex, crypto, and fixed-income securities.

The platform uses a straightforward four-tier pricing model with no surprises: a free tier that actually delivers value, plus three paid plans ranging from $25 to $119 per month. Unlike many fintech platforms, Koyfin doesn't lock core functionality behind paywalls—the free tier is positioned as a legitimate entry point, while paid tiers unlock advanced analytics, portfolio tools, and premium data feeds.

Starting price: Free (or $25/month for Plus tier)

All Koyfin Plans Compared

Koyfin's pricing tiers are designed to scale with your needs, from casual market watchers to professional analysts. Here's the complete breakdown:

Plan Monthly Price Best For
Free $0 Casual investors, market research, learning the platform
Plus $25/month Active individual investors, basic portfolio tracking
Pro $55/month Serious traders, fundamental analysts, portfolio managers
Premier $119/month Professional advisors, wealth managers, institutional users

Note: Koyfin does not publicly disclose a detailed feature breakdown for each tier on their pricing page. Based on typical SaaS models and the platform's positioning, premium tiers likely include enhanced portfolio analytics, advanced alerts, additional workspace customization, and priority support. For exact feature availability in each plan, check Koyfin's current pricing page or contact their sales team directly.

The price progression reveals the target audience: the Plus tier ($25) is designed for serious individual investors who want more than the free tier but aren't professionals. The Pro tier ($55) represents the sweet spot for active traders and fundamental analysts. Premier ($119) is pitched at financial advisors and wealth managers who need advanced client portfolio tools and white-glove support.

Free Plan / Free Trial

Koyfin's free tier is one of the platform's strongest selling points. Unlike many competitors that offer gimped demo versions, the free plan provides genuine utility:

  • Full access to charting and data across stocks, ETFs, forex, crypto, and fixed-income markets
  • Fundamental analysis tools: earnings data, financial statements, valuation metrics
  • Macroeconomic data: interest rates, inflation indicators, economic calendars
  • Customizable watchlists and dashboards
  • News feed integration
  • Mobile app access (with limitations compared to web platform)

The free tier is best for:

  • Casual investors who want to research companies and track markets without paying
  • Students and beginners learning financial analysis
  • Market researchers needing historical data and macro context
  • Long-term buy-and-hold investors who don't need real-time alerts or premium analytics

Is the free plan worth it? Yes, absolutely. If you're not actively trading or managing client portfolios, the free tier likely covers 80% of what you need. Most retail investors using Koyfin for research—not execution—will find they never need to upgrade.

Hidden Costs and Fees

Koyfin's pricing is transparent: you pay the monthly subscription, and that's it. There are no hidden costs for:

  • Market data feeds (included in all plans)
  • Exchange fees (Koyfin covers these)
  • Platform access or concurrent user limits (at least not at the individual tier)
  • Mobile app usage

What isn't included: Koyfin is a data and analytics tool, not a broker. You cannot execute trades directly from the platform, so you'll need a separate brokerage account (which may have its own commissions, depending on your broker). Similarly, Koyfin does not offer backtesting or automated trading—those features are found on platforms like TradingView or more specialized tools.

Koyfin Pricing vs Competitors

How does Koyfin stack up against alternatives? Here's a quick comparison:

Platform Free Tier Most Popular Paid Tier Strengths
Koyfin Yes Pro ($55/mo) Deep fundamental data, macro indicators, global markets, strong free tier
TradingView Yes Pro ($15/mo) Superior charting, backtesting, large trading community, more affordable
Bloomberg Terminal No ~$24,000/year Industry standard, unmatched institutional data (but overkill and unaffordable for retail)

Koyfin vs TradingView: TradingView is cheaper ($15/month for Pro vs. $55/month) and has superior charting and backtesting tools. However, TradingView's fundamental data is basic compared to Koyfin's institutional-quality financials and macro coverage. If charting is your priority, TradingView wins. If fundamental analysis and macroeconomic context matter more, Koyfin is worth the extra cost.

Koyfin vs Bloomberg Terminal: Bloomberg costs ~$24,000 per year ($2,000/month), making Koyfin's $119/month Premier tier roughly 1/17th the price. Koyfin doesn't have Bloomberg's depth for institutional traders, but for retail investors and independent advisors, Koyfin delivers 90% of the value at a fraction of the cost.

See detailed comparisons: Koyfin vs TradingView | Full Koyfin Review

Is Koyfin Worth the Price?

For the Free Tier: Absolutely. If you do any amount of investment research, the free tier pays for itself immediately. The data quality, charting, and fundamental analysis tools rival paid services costing 10x more elsewhere.

For the Plus Tier ($25/month): Worth it if you actively track a portfolio and want better alerts, advanced watchlists, and portfolio analytics. This is the entry point for "serious hobby investors" who spend more than 5 hours per week on research or trading.

For the Pro Tier ($55/month): This is the sweet spot for most retail investors. At $55/month, you're paying ~$1.83 per day for tools that institutional analysts use. If you manage your own money seriously, day trade, or spend significant time on fundamental analysis, this tier pays for itself in better decision-making. Upgrading from Plus ($25) to Pro ($55) adds $30/month—a reasonable jump for professionals.

For the Premier Tier ($119/month): This is for financial advisors managing client portfolios, wealth managers, and investment professionals. At this price point, you're competing with Bloomberg Terminal (which costs 20x more), so the ROI is clear if you're using Koyfin in a professional capacity. For individual traders, this tier is overkill.

When to upgrade:

  • Free → Plus: When you have $5,000+ under management or trade weekly
  • Plus → Pro: When you're making trading/investment decisions that could cost/earn >$5,000/month (where $30 subscription becomes negligible)
  • Pro → Premier: Only if you're a professional advisor with clients (rarely for retail)

How to Save on Koyfin

Annual Billing: Koyfin typically offers discounts for annual prepayment. While exact discounts vary, expect 10-20% savings if you commit to a full year. This would bring Plus down from $300/year to ~$240-270/year, and Pro from $660/year to ~$528-594/year.

Student Plans: Check with Koyfin directly about student discounts. Many fintech platforms offer reduced rates for verified students, though this isn't prominently advertised.

Company/Team Plans: If you're part of an investment firm or advisory business, Koyfin may offer bulk pricing or team accounts at a discount. Contact their sales team to discuss options.

Free Tier Forever: The most obvious money-saver: use the free tier as long as possible. For many retail investors, free + occasional Plus upgrade months during high-activity periods beats paying $25/month year-round.

Sign up for promotions: Koyfin occasionally runs limited-time offers (especially during market volatility or product launches). Following their Twitter/LinkedIn or signing up for email alerts can surface discounts.

See the full Koyfin review for more details on features and user experience.
author: TradingToolsHub Editorial
